### Post by starNIX on 2006-02-04
Ok,  I am at my wits end here.  Ive tried everything I can find in the forums and nothing.  

What I am trying to do is run 1920x1200 on my monitor attached to my nVidia card and I want to run 1024x768 on the monitor attached to the onboard Intel chip.

Here is my current xorg.conf.  Can anyone see anything wrong?  


# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the /etc/X11/xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man /etc/X11/xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "Files"
    FontPath    "/usr/share/X11/fonts/misc"
    FontPath    "/usr/share/X11/fonts/cyrillic"
    FontPath    "/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi/:unscaled"
    FontPath    "/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi/:unscaled"
    FontPath    "/usr/share/X11/fonts/Type1"
    FontPath    "/usr/share/X11/fonts/CID"
    FontPath    "/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi"
    FontPath    "/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi"
        # paths to defoma fonts
    FontPath    "/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/TrueType"
    FontPath    "/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/CID"
EndSection

Section "Module"
    #Load    "GLcore"
    Load    "bitmap"
    Load    "ddc"
    #Load    "dri"
    Load    "extmod"
    Load    "freetype"
    Load    "glx"
    Load    "int10"
    Load    "type1"
    Load    "vbe"
EndSection

Section "Extensions"
    Option "Composite" "Enable"
        Option "RENDER" "true"
 #       Option "DAMAGE" "true"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
    Identifier    "Generic Keyboard"
    Driver        "kbd"
    Option        "CoreKeyboard"
    Option        "XkbRules"    "xorg"
    Option        "XkbModel"    "pc104"
    Option        "XkbLayout"    "us"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
    Identifier    "Configured Mouse"
    Driver        "mouse"
    Option        "CorePointer"
    Option        "Device"        "/dev/input/mice"
    Option        "Protocol"        "ImPS/2"
    Option        "ZAxisMapping"        "4 5"
EndSection

Section "Device"
    Identifier    "Quadro4"
    Driver        "nvidia"
    BusID        "PCI:1:0:0"
    #VideoRam    65536
    Option        "UseFBDev"        "true"
    Option        "RenderAccel"        "true"
    Option        "AllowGLXWithComposite"    "true"
    Screen 0
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
    Identifier    "Monitor 1"
    Option        "DPMS"
    HorizSync 30-121
    VertRefresh 48-160
EndSection

Section "Screen"
    Identifier    "nVidia Screen"
    Device        "Quadro4"
    Monitor        "Monitor 1"
    DefaultDepth    16
    SubSection "Display"
        Depth        1
        Modes        "1920x1200" "1600x1200" "1280x1024" "1280x960" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
    EndSubSection
    SubSection "Display"
        Depth        4
        Modes        "1920x1200" "1600x1200" "1280x1024" "1280x960" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
    EndSubSection
    SubSection "Display"
        Depth        8

    EndSubSection
    SubSection "Display"

        Modes        "1920x1200" "1600x1200" "1280x1024" "1280x960" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
    EndSubSection
    SubSection "Display"
        Depth        16
        Modes        "1920x1200" "1600x1200" "1280x1024" "1280x960" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
    EndSubSection
    SubSection "Display"
        Depth        24
        Modes        "1920x1200" "1600x1200" "1280x1024" "1280x960" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
    EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Device"
    Identifier    "i810"
    Driver        "i810"
    BusID        "PCI:0:2:0"
    VideoRam    65536
    Option        "UseFBDev"        "true"
    Screen 1
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
    Identifier    "Monitor 2"
    Option        "DPMS"
    HorizSync    28-49
    VertRefresh    43-72
EndSection

Section "Screen"
    Identifier    "i810 Screen"
    Device        "i810"
    Monitor        "Monitor 2"
    DefaultDepth    16
    SubSection "Display"
        Depth        1
        Modes        "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
    EndSubSection
    SubSection "Display"
        Depth        4
        Modes        "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
    EndSubSection

        Modes        "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"

        Modes        "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
    EndSubSection
    SubSection "Display"
        Depth        16
        Modes        "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
    EndSubSection
    SubSection "Display"
        Depth        24
        Modes        "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
    EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerFlags" 
    Option "Xinerama" "ON"
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
    Identifier    "Dual Head Layout"
    Screen 0     "nVidia Screen" 0 0
        Screen 1     "i810 Screen" LeftOf "nVidia Screen"
    InputDevice    "Generic Keyboard"
    InputDevice    "Configured Mouse"
EndSection

Section "DRI"
    Mode    0666
EndSection

### Post by Ocxic on 2006-02-04
generally I ahve discoverd the mainboards of most computer automatically disable on-board video when a video is inserted into an AGP/PCI slot, this may or may not be the case for you, but I have never been able to get my onboard video wroking with another video card installed.

anyway, try changing your "Primary video on boot" (or something to this extent) option from AGP to PCI in your bios, tho doing this has broken my xserver b4.

---

### Post by starNIX on 2006-02-04
I tried that and then the onboard one worked but the PCI one didnt.  So there is no way to make this work?  I only have one accessable slot so I cant put in another card.  :-(

### Post by Ocxic on 2006-02-04
from the way you discribe it, it seems that you can only use one of them at a time. oe option is to get another video card, and use that, to get your 2 screen going

### Post by starNIX on 2006-02-04
Crap...  The case on this computer for some reason blocks 3 of the PCI slots with the power supply.  is there a way to make a DVI video card run 2 screens with only one connector?  I have a low profile case and only one PCI slot with an NVidia 4800 in it.

Any chance of getting 2 screens working?
